title: Occupational Licensing Around the World
summary:
Hartley and Kleiner have a new Fed Minneapolis working paper surveying workers around the world to measure occupational licensing by country. Their headline result is the opposite: occupational licensing is negatively correlated with GDP per […]

title: Incentives matter, installment #1637
summary:

We model this decision as a metric-driven principal-agent problem […]

title: Persistent Inequality in Publishing in Economics
summary:
This paper documents new facts about concentration in publishing in economics. First, the profession grows downward . The number of economists grew almost sixfold since 1990, but new entrants publish in lower-tier journals while incumbents hold the top. Second, there is high and persistent concentration at the top. Along with the downward growth, the top-1% […]

title: Chinese gem lab rakes in profits as world embraces artificial diamonds
summary:
A Chinese lab-grown diamond producer saw its profits triple over the first half of the year, as consumers around the world increasingly abandon natural diamonds in favour of cheaper artificial stones.

title: China’s first-tier home prices extend 4-month rebound amid market stabilisation
summary:
New home prices in four Chinese first-tier cities rose by an average of 0.1 per cent last month, extending a four-month rebound and signalling a gradual recovery in market sentiment as housing stabilisation measures gain traction.
Compared with May, home prices edged up 0.3 per cent in Shanghai and Shenzhen and by 0.2 per cent in Guangzhou, according to data released by the National Bureau of Statistics on Wednesday. However, they fell 0.3 per cent in Beijing.
